Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Claude
Message:
feat(whatsapp): Dealix WhatsApp Client OS — client-facing governed surface

A menu-driven business workflow assistant over WhatsApp (NOT a general-purpose
chatbot), distinct from the internal founder-only whatsapp_decision_bot. Guides
clients: readiness scan → catalog-tied recommendation → permissions → drafts →
proposal → proof → payment/onboarding → support → human handoff.

- auto_client_acquisition/whatsapp_client_os/: engine, deterministic intent
  router, conversation state machine, 10-axis readiness assessment, L0–L5
  permission ladder + guard, policy guard (secrets-in-chat + unsafe-request
  blocking, wrapping channel_policy_gateway + safe_send doctrine), action-card
  builder, handoff router, JSONL store, metrics, templates.
- api/routers/whatsapp_client_os.py (/api/v1/whatsapp-client-os) wired into app
  via the defensive optional-router pattern.
- data/whatsapp/ (templates + assessment questions); JSON Schemas under
  dealix/contracts/schemas/ with a regen script.
- Founder ops UI: /[locale]/ops/whatsapp (+ sessions, action-cards, assessments).
- docs/whatsapp/ (12 bilingual files); reports/whatsapp/ + verify/report scripts.

59 tests enforce the doctrine: no secrets in chat (secure portal links), no
cold WhatsApp / blasts / scraping / LinkedIn automation, human approval for
external sends, human handoff for ambiguity/sensitive-data/pricing/contracts/
complaints, and every recommendation tied to service_catalog + an evidence level.

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Claude
Message:
feat(autonomy): Phase 0-2 foundation for autonomous distribution

Lands the foundation of the 12-week autonomous distribution plan
(/root/.claude/plans/typed-brewing-pebble.md):

Phase 0 — Foundation:
- Settings extended with moyasar_mode/live_verified gate, scheduler_backend,
  KSA residency enforcement, email deliverability attestation, SBOM key id,
  and per-phase self-serve feature flags (all default OFF / safest).
- MoyasarClient hardened with MoyasarLiveGateError on any live charge unless
  mode==live AND live_verified==True (Non-Negotiable #6).
- MoyasarClient.verify_account_status() probes activation with 5-min cache.
- MoyasarClient.create_hosted_payment() — Source Passport required (#10).
- scripts/secrets_rotation_audit.py: read-only audit, exits non-zero when
  critical secrets are missing in production.
- DEALIX_READINESS.md: end-to-end Moyasar live activation runbook.

Phase 1 — Autonomous Lead-to-Cash:
- SelfServeIntakeGuard composes forbidden_actions + claim_safety +
  lawful_basis into a single bilingual (AR/EN) verdict.
- 5 productized offers catalog; auto-approve class limited to the 3 lowest
  tiers (Diagnostic, 499 Sprint, 1,500 Data Pack).
- OfferCheckoutService: deterministic SHA-256 idempotency key per
  (passport_id, offer_id); never mints a Moyasar invoice for escalation
  classes; closed-loop service with no external sends.
- api/routers/offers_checkout.py: public POST /v1/offers/{id}/checkout,
  gated by Settings.offers_self_serve_enabled (returns 503 when off).

Phase 2 — Sprint Delivery Workflow:
- Ed25519 (with HMAC-fallback) signer; refuses dev key in production.
- SprintWorkflow: 11-step deterministic pipeline; idempotent resume from
  any step on retry; sign step refuses single-locale output (#9).

Tests: 84 new tests, all green. Existing doctrine guards regress clean.

Description: Found a Stripe Access Token, posing a risk to payment processing services and sensitive financial data.
Author: Claude
Message:
feat(ops): Dealix Master Verification System (9-layer wrapper)

Adds a single command (`make verify-all`) that aggregates a yes/no verdict
across 9 layers: repo structure, code health, data contracts, prompt/output
quality, trust & security, revenue runtime, server runtime, GitHub governance,
and business evidence (including optional $PRIVATE_OPS CSV contracts).

The system wraps existing tooling rather than duplicating it: the 75+ existing
verify_*/check_*/audit_* scripts, the 8 doctrine tests (tests/test_no_*.py),
the dealix.classifications (A0-A3/R0-R3/S0-S3) and DecisionOutput contract
(dealix/contracts/decision.py:118-127 high-stakes validator), the 5 YAML evals,
and the existing master verifiers (v10_master_verify.sh, revenue_os_master_verify.sh)
all remain authoritative.

Exit-code contract: 0=PASS, 1=FAIL, 2=PARTIAL (e.g. PRIVATE_OPS unset).
Master aggregation: any 1 -> FAIL; only 2 -> PARTIAL; all 0 -> PASS.

Files:
- scripts/master_verify.py (orchestrator with JSON report)
- scripts/verify_layer{1..9}_*.{py,sh}
- docs/ops/MASTER_VERIFICATION_SYSTEM.md
- docs/evals/PROMPT_OUTPUT_EVAL_MATRIX.md
- .github/workflows/dealix-master-verify.yml (PR + push gate)
- Makefile: verify-all, verify-layer-%
- .env.example: PRIVATE_OPS=/opt/dealix-ops-private (external mount)
